attempts.strategy: Mathlib-shape Derivation distribution lemma; one-line proof via ext + simp only [postcomp_d_apply, comp_app] + rfl
attempts.code_tried:
@[simp]
lemma _root_.PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_comp
    {C : Type*} [Category C] {D : Type*} [Category D]
    {F : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat}
    {M N P : _root_.PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
    {φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R}
    (d : M.Derivation φ) (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ P) :
    d.postcomp (f ≫ g) = (d.postcomp f).postcomp g := by
  ext X b
  simp only [_root_.PresheafOfModules.Derivation.postcomp_d_apply,
             _root_.PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
  rfl
attempts.insight: `_root_.PresheafOfModules` disambiguation needed throughout — inside the Scheme namespace, `PresheafOfModules` refers to project-local `Scheme.PresheafOfModules`. `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp]` does not fire on the residual `(f.app X ≫ g.app X).hom (...)` because the LHS does not present in the rewriter's expected shape, but rfl recovers — composition in ModuleCat IS LinearMap.comp at the .hom level.

findings.blocker: After `apply SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` + `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective`, the goal is per-U surjectivity of `(d1.app U.unop).desc.hom`. Attempting `Submodule.mem_top y` fails to synthesize `Module ↑(X.presheaf.obj U) ↑((relativeDifferentials f).val.obj U)` because the natural module instance is over `(X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj U` (the forget₂-image), not directly over `X.presheaf.obj U`. The two are the same underlying ring but different categorical types, and instance synthesis does not bridge them.
